docs.title: Overview
docs.text: Vexy Lines for Mac & Windows | Download | Buy | Batch GUI | CLI/MCP | API | .lines format

docs.title: vexy-lines-run
docs.text:
Desktop GUI for Vexy Lines style transfer.

Load images, .lines files, or video. Pick a style. Export. Built with CustomTkinter -- runs on macOS, Windows, and Linux wherever Tk is available.

docs.title: What it does
docs.text:
A three-tab desktop app that applies Vexy Lines artistic styles to any input:

  • Lines tab -- load .lines files, export previews or apply a new style
  • Images tab -- load PNG, JPG, WEBP rasters, style them via the MCP API
  • Video tab -- load MP4, MOV, MKV, process frame-by-frame with style transfer and audio passthrough

Select a primary style from any .lines file. Optionally select an end style -- the two interpolate linearly across the input sequence for smooth transitions.

Export runs on a background thread. The progress bar updates live. The UI stays responsive.

docs.title: Quick start
docs.text:
pip install vexy-lines-run
vexy-lines-gui

Or from Python:

from vexy_lines_run import launch
launch()

docs.title: Next steps
docs.text:
• Installation -- install options and optional extras
  • GUI Guide -- walkthrough of every UI section
  • API Reference -- Python API for the app and processing modules
  • Examples -- usage patterns and workflows

docs.title: App(CTk)
docs.text:
The main GUI application window. Subclass of customtkinter.CTk.

from vexy_lines_run import App

app = App()
app.mainloop()

Creates the three-tab layout, style pickers, export controls, and progress bar. Export runs on a daemon thread via processing.process_export().

docs.title: launch() -> None
docs.text:
Convenience function that creates an App instance and calls mainloop().

from vexy_lines_run import launch

launch()

docs.title: process_export(...) -> None
docs.text:
Background thread dispatcher for all export modes. Called internally by App when the Export button is pressed.

Handles three input types:

  • Lines files -- extracts previews or applies styles depending on configuration
  • Image files -- applies style via MCP, exports as SVG/PNG/JPG
  • Video files -- per-frame style transfer with PyAV decode/encode

Parameters are passed via the App instance's current state (selected files, style, format, DPI, etc.).

Progress callbacks (on_progress, on_complete, on_error) are invoked on the background thread. The App marshals them to the main thread via self.after(0, callback).

docs.title: Video
docs.text: Requires the [video] extra: PyAV, OpenCV, resvg-py, svglab.

docs.title: VideoInfo
docs.text:
Dataclass with video metadata.

Field Type Description width int Frame width in pixels height int Frame height in pixels fps float Frames per second total_frames int Total frame count duration float Duration in seconds has_audio bool Whether an audio stream exists

docs.title: probe(path) -> VideoInfo
docs.text:
Read video metadata without decoding frames.

from vexy_lines_run.video import probe

info = probe("clip.mp4")
print(f"{info.width}x{info.height} @ {info.fps}fps, {info.total_frames} frames")

docs.title: process_video(...) -> VideoInfo
docs.text:
Decode a video, process each frame through a callback, and re-encode to a new video.

from vexy_lines_run.video import process_video

def frame_callback(frame_index, total_frames):
    return {"angle": 45, "interval": 2.0}

info = process_video(
    input_path="clip.mp4",
    output_path="result.mp4",
    frame_params=frame_callback,
    max_frames=100,
    host="127.0.0.1",
    port=47384,
    dpi=72,
)

docs.title: CTkRangeSlider
docs.text:
A dual-handle range slider built on CustomTkinter. Used in the Video tab for selecting start/end frame ranges.

from vexy_lines_run.widgets import CTkRangeSlider

slider = CTkRangeSlider(
    parent,
    from_=0,
    to=100,
    command=lambda low, high: print(f"Range: {low}-{high}"),
)
slider.pack()
Parameter Type Description from_ float Minimum value to float Maximum value command callable Called with (low, high) on change

docs.title: Changelog
docs.text: See CHANGELOG.md for the full release history.

docs.title: Launch the GUI
docs.text:
From the terminal:

vexy-lines-gui

From Python:

from vexy_lines_run import launch
launch()

As a module:

python -m vexy_lines_run

docs.title: Typical workflow: style transfer on images
docs.text:
1. Launch the GUI
  2. Switch to the Images tab
  3. Click "Add Files" and select your photos
  4. Click "Select Style" and pick a .lines file
  5. Choose output format (SVG, PNG, or JPG)
  6. Click "Export"
  7. Watch the progress bar -- the UI stays responsive

docs.title: Style interpolation across a sequence
docs.text:
1. Load a sequence of images (e.g. video frames exported as JPGs)
  2. Select a primary style (the starting look)
  3. Select an end style (the finishing look)
  4. Export -- the first image gets 100% primary, the last gets 100% end, everything in between blends smoothly

docs.title: Video processing
docs.text:
1. Install the video extra: pip install "vexy-lines-run[video]"
  2. Switch to the Video tab
  3. Load a video file
  4. Select a style
  5. Optionally set start/end frame range with the dual-handle slider
  6. Toggle audio passthrough
  7. Click "Export" -- each frame is decoded, styled, and re-encoded

docs.title: Probe video metadata
docs.text:
from vexy_lines_run.video import probe

info = probe("clip.mp4")
print(f"Resolution: {info.width}x{info.height}")
print(f"FPS: {info.fps}")
print(f"Frames: {info.total_frames}")
print(f"Duration: {info.duration:.1f}s")
print(f"Audio: {'yes' if info.has_audio else 'no'}")

docs.title: Drag-and-drop
docs.text:
Install the [dnd] extra:

pip install "vexy-lines-run[dnd]"

Now drop files directly onto the input list in any tab. The app filters by file type based on the active tab.

docs.title: Use the range slider widget standalone
docs.text:
import customtkinter as ctk
from vexy_lines_run.widgets import CTkRangeSlider

root = ctk.CTk()
root.geometry("400x100")

def on_range_change(low, high):
    print(f"Selected range: {low:.0f} - {high:.0f}")

slider = CTkRangeSlider(root, from_=0, to=300, command=on_range_change)
slider.pack(padx=20, pady=20, fill="x")

root.mainloop()

docs.title: GUI Guide
docs.text: The Vexy Lines GUI is a CustomTkinter desktop app with three sections: input selection, style pickers, and export controls.

docs.title: Lines tab
docs.text:
Load one or more .lines files. Each file appears in a scrollable list showing its filename. Use the "Add Files" button or drag-and-drop (requires [dnd] extra).

When you export from this tab:

  • If a style is selected, the style's fill structure is applied to each file's source image
  • If no style is selected, the embedded preview image is extracted directly

docs.title: Images tab
docs.text:
Load raster images: PNG, JPG, JPEG, WEBP, BMP, TIFF, and others supported by Pillow.

A style must be selected. Each image is opened as a new document in Vexy Lines via MCP, the style's fill tree is replicated, the document is rendered, and the result is exported.

docs.title: Video tab
docs.text:
Load video files: MP4, MOV, MKV, AVI, and others supported by PyAV.

Requires the [video] extra. Style transfer runs per-frame: each frame is decoded, styled via MCP, and re-encoded. Audio is passed through when available.

The dual-handle range slider lets you select a frame range (start/end) instead of processing the entire video.

docs.title: Primary style
docs.text:
Click "Select Style" to choose a .lines file. The picker shows an inline thumbnail preview extracted from the file's embedded preview image.

The primary style defines the fill structure applied to every input.

docs.title: End style (optional)
docs.text:
Click "Select End Style" for a second .lines file. When set, the two styles are interpolated linearly across the input sequence:

  • First input gets 100% primary style
  • Last input gets 100% end style
  • Inputs in between are blended proportionally

Both styles must have compatible structures (same groups, layers, fills, fill types). The app checks compatibility and warns if they don't match.

docs.title: Format selector
docs.text: Format Notes SVG Vector output from the style engine PNG Raster with optional 2x upscale JPG Raster with optional 2x upscale MP4 Re-encoded video (Video tab only) LINES Copy .lines files directly (Lines tab only)

docs.title: Size options
docs.text: For raster and video exports, choose the output resolution. The "2x" option doubles the dimensions for higher-quality output.

docs.title: Audio passthrough
docs.text: For video exports, toggle whether to copy the audio track from the input video to the output.

docs.title: Export button
docs.text: Starts the export. Processing runs on a daemon thread -- the UI stays responsive. The progress bar shows completion percentage. Cancel at any time.

docs.title: Progress feedback
docs.text:
Three callbacks drive the UI:

  • on_progress(current, total) -- updates the progress bar
  • on_complete(results) -- shows a completion dialog with file counts
  • on_error(message) -- shows an error dialog

All callbacks are marshalled to the Tk main thread via self.after(0, ...).

docs.title: Drag-and-drop
docs.text: With the [dnd] extra installed, drop files directly onto any input list. The app accepts .lines files (Lines tab), image files (Images tab), and video files (Video tab) based on the active tab.

docs.title: Dark/light mode
docs.text: CustomTkinter follows the system appearance by default. The app uses the slate dark theme and deep purple accent colour.

docs.title: Keyboard shortcuts
docs.text: Shortcut Action Cmd/Ctrl+O Open file dialog for the active tab Cmd/Ctrl+E Start export Escape Cancel running export

docs.title: Requirements
docs.text:
• Python 3.11 or newer
  • Tk/Tcl (ships with most Python installs; on Linux, install python3-tk)
  • The Vexy Lines desktop app for style transfer via MCP

docs.title: Install from PyPI
docs.text:
pip install vexy-lines-run

Or with uv:

uv add vexy-lines-run

docs.title: Optional extras
docs.text: Extra What it adds Install dnd Drag-and-drop file support pip install "vexy-lines-run[dnd]" video Video processing (PyAV, OpenCV, resvg, svglab) pip install "vexy-lines-run[video]" menus Native menu bar pip install "vexy-lines-run[menus]" all Everything above pip install "vexy-lines-run[all]"

docs.title: Runtime dependencies
docs.text: Package Why vexy-lines-apy MCP client and style engine customtkinter Modern-looking Tk widgets Pillow Image loading and thumbnail generation loguru Structured debug logging

docs.title: Launch
docs.text:
Three ways to start the GUI:

# Installed script
vexy-lines-gui

# Module entry point
python -m vexy_lines_run

# From Python
from vexy_lines_run import launch
launch()

docs.title: Verify the install
docs.text:
vexy-lines-gui --help

Or:

from vexy_lines_run import App
print("vexy-lines-run is ready")

docs.title: Platform notes
docs.text:
macOS: Works out of the box. Drag-and-drop requires the [dnd] extra.

Windows: Works out of the box. Drag-and-drop requires the [dnd] extra.

Linux: Install python3-tk from your package manager if Tk is not bundled with your Python install. Drag-and-drop requires [dnd] plus tkdnd system library.

docs.title: Development install
docs.text:
git clone https://github.com/vexyart/vexy-lines.git
cd vexy-lines/vexy-lines-run
uv venv --python 3.12
uv pip install -e ".[all,dev]"

Run tests:

uvx hatch test
